title: NOC 83110 – Logging machinery operators | GTR Immigration
description: Logging machinery operators under NOC 83110 are the technical drivers of Canada's massive forestry sector. As a TEER 3 occupation, these professionals operate the high-tech, heavy-duty machinery required to fell, delimb, and transport timber in managed forests. In 2026, as Canada shifts toward "Precision Forestry" and sustainable harvesting to combat climate change, these operators are more essential than ever. For immigration, this role is a primary candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the Express Entry Category-Based Selection for Trade occupations.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level3Major Group83 – Occupations in natural resources and related productionSub-Major Group831 – Occupations in natural resources and related productionMinor Group8311 – Logging machinery operatorsNOC Code83110

duties: Operate heavy-duty logging machinery (e.g., feller-bunchers, harvesters, processors) to fell, delimb, and buck trees.
Drive and operate skidders or forwarders to transport logs from the felling site to a central loading area or landing.
Operate grapple yarders or cable systems to move logs across difficult terrain or steep slopes.
Perform routine maintenance on heavy logging equipment, such as cleaning, lubricating, and making minor repairs.
Ensure strict adherence to environmental regulations regarding soil disturbance and wildlife habitat protection.
Coordinate with ground crews and supervisors using radio communications to ensure safe and efficient operations.
Maintain production records and report any technical or mechanical issues to maintenance staff.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience as a logging or forestry worker are usually mandatory.
On-the-Job Training: Extensive training (up to several months) on specific machinery is standard practice.

Certifications:A provincial heavy equipment operator's license or specific forestry equipment certification may be required.First Aid Certification is mandatory for remote woodland operations.A valid driver's license (often Class 1 or 3) is usually required for transporting machinery.
A provincial heavy equipment operator's license or specific forestry equipment certification may be required.
First Aid Certification is mandatory for remote woodland operations.
A valid driver's license (often Class 1 or 3) is usually required for transporting machinery.
Physical stamina, high mechanical aptitude, and the ability to work in remote, outdoor environments in all weather conditions are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced operators in B.C. or Alberta can earn annual salaries exceeding $105,000, especially when including overtime and remote site premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), a total of 2,200 job openings are projected through 2033, driven largely by the retirement of the current workforce. As the industry moves toward high-tech, lower-impact harvesting, the need for skilled operators who can handle complex, computer-integrated machinery will continue to grow.
labour_market_demand: The demand for logging machinery operators is Good to Very Good, particularly in resource-rich provinces like British Columbia, Quebec, and New Brunswick. In 2026, the industry is seeing a significant surge in demand for operators trained in "Smart Logging" technology. With a high volume of retirements expected over the next five years, Canadian forestry firms are actively seeking international talent with mechanical aptitude and experience in remote woodland operations.
Global Forestry Leader: Work with the world's most advanced harvesting technology in a country recognized for its sustainable forest management practices.
Technological Innovation: Gain experience with GPS-guided feller-bunchers, automated harvesters, and real-time timber volume tracking software.
High Compensation: This is a high-paying skilled trade, often featuring "Camp Job" benefits, including housing and meal allowances in remote regions.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC is a high priority for Express Entry Category-Based Draws for "Trade Occupations" and the BC PNP Skills Immigration stream.
pathways: Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable route for operators with the required work experience and a job offer.
Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted specifically for candidates in the "Trade Occupations" category.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure for workers already in Canada contributing to essential sectors like natural resources and forestry.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: A high-priority pathway for operators supporting the province's vital timber industry.
